mnemonics: SCAM G (Summarize, Categorize, Aggregate, Manipulate - Grouped)
keyPoints: Aggregate functions (e.g., SUM, AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN) perform calculations on a set of values and return a single value.
The GROUP BY clause is used with aggregate functions to group rows that have the same values into summary rows.
The HAVING clause is used to filter groups based on a condition, similar to WHERE but for grouped data.
commonMistakes: Using an aggregate function in a WHERE clause (must use HAVING for grouped conditions).
Omitting a non-aggregated column from the GROUP BY clause when selecting it.
Confusing COUNT(column_name) which counts non-null values with COUNT(*) which counts all rows.
